---
layout: post
title: "The Model That Ate What You Fed It"
subtitle: "HTB SmartHire, where an AI hiring platform hides a model server behind a second name, the model server still wears the login it shipped with, and the model itself is a recipe of actions you get to rewrite"
date: 2026-07-17 12:00:00 +0000
description: "HTB SmartHire walkthrough. A medium Linux box: a hidden MLflow server with default creds, a model pickle you overwrite for RCE, blind egress, and a .pth line root runs. Sealed while the box is live."
image: /assets/og/smarthire.png
tags: [hackthebox, writeup]
---

SmartHire sells the dream of an AI that reads resumes so a human never has to, and the dream is a thin coat of paint over a machine-learning platform doing the real work behind it. The front-end tells on that platform in the words it uses, and a second name fuzzed out of the same IP leads you straight to it. The model server there never changed the login it shipped with, and the models it stores are not data at all. They are pickles, which means they are recipes of actions that get run the moment something loads them. The whole box is what happens when a platform trusts a stranger to feed it something inert and gets handed code instead. And when the machine will not phone home, you stop asking it to speak and make it leave notes in a room you already hold a key to.

```
        s m a r t h i r e        (medium · linux)
        =============================
        the front door sells you an AI.
        the back door runs the model.
        the back door kept its factory lock.
                        |
                        v
        the model is not a file you read.
        it is a recipe the box cooks on load,
        so you rewrite the recipe.
                        |
                        v
        the box will not call you back, so you
        have it leave the answer in its own store.
                                              . . .
```

The full chain, from the second vhost to host root, is written up below in the usual detail. Because SmartHire is **still active on Hack The Box**, that write-up is sealed. It unseals the moment the box retires. If you already hold the passphrase, the gate is right here.

<script type="application/json" id="lock-data">{"v":1,"iter":250000,"salt":"XJmxqb41HpfmbooATctEXQ==","iv":"aigFt01LaJwu1nf+","ct":""}</script>

---

*HTB: SmartHire, a medium Linux box that is really a lecture on shipped defaults, why a model artifact is executable code, and the difference between silence on the wire and silence on disk. Still active. The full chain unseals here the day it retires, no live payloads either way.*
